trigger: 115000061 review — Fourier bet 0/5 miss, hypothesize Fourier weak signal detection
design: ACB bet1 + Markov bet2 + Conditional(Fourier>0.40→Fourier, else→LagEcho) bet3
failure_reason: NO-OP — Fourier max_score almost ALWAYS exceeds 0.40 in 539 (5804-draw FFT has stable spectral representation for all numbers). The LagEcho fallback path triggers in only ~1/1500 draws. All four thresholds (0.25-0.40) produce nearly identical results to pure Fourier. Conditional mechanism design is valid but premise is wrong: Fourier signal is 'always on' in 539.
key_insight: 539 has 5804+ daily draws — FFT window=500 always has rich data. Unlike lower-frequency lotteries, there is no 'Fourier dead zone'. Conditional switching is unnecessary.
retest_conditions: If applied to lower-frequency lottery (e.g., Big Lotto with weekly draws) where FFT may genuinely have weak periods
